meta.notes: DRAFT of the ELW Night Prayer (Compline) ordo. Structure verified against the authoritative Sundays & Seasons ordo (docs/research/2026-07-liturgy-strategy/12-sns-verification.md): Opening -> Confession -> Psalmody (Ps 91/134) -> office hymn -> short Reading -> responsory ('Into thy hands') -> Nunc Dimittis -> Prayers (intercessions / Lord's Prayer / collect) -> Blessing. The spurious threefold Kyrie has been removed (no Kyrie in the S&S Night Prayer ordo); an office hymn added between Psalmody and the Reading; intercessions modeled as a prayers_of_intercession proper_slot. OPEN owner/legal calls from doc 12 not yet applied: ELW's Compline confession Option B may sit closer to PD than the 1662 substitute now bundled (legal check); the blessing fallback reuses the opening 'quiet night' text rather than either distinct S&S option; collect modeled day-variable vs. S&S's fixed alternatives. TEXT PROVENANCE per docs/research/2026-07-liturgy-strategy/10-pd-text-sourcing.md: bundles the traditional PUBLIC-DOMAIN default text (traditional Compline opening/blessing 'The Lord Almighty grant us a quiet night and a perfect end', 1662 BCP General Confession, traditional 'Into thy hands' responsory from Ps 31:5, KJV Nunc Dimittis, ancient threefold Kyrie, traditional 'Our Father'). IMPORTANT (doc 10 flag #3): the 1662 General Confession is a WORKING PD SUBSTITUTE for ELW's Compline confession, NOT verbatim ELW. ELW's contemporary wording (ELW's confession, ELW's responsory, the ELLC Nunc Dimittis 'Now, Lord, you let your servant go in peace', the collect, and ELW's exact blessing 'quiet night and peace at the last') is COPYRIGHTED and NOT bundled: left as [VAR]/[LIC] slots resolved via entitlement. Element status: opening=[PD], confession=[PD 1662, substitute], psalmody=[VAR], reading=[VAR], responsory=[PD], Nunc Dimittis=[PD KJV], Kyrie=[PD], Lord's Prayer=[PD], collect=[VAR/LIC], blessing=[PD].

blocks.note: Composite cover page from CongregationProfile. Identity/chrome, not liturgical text.

blocks.title: *OPENING
blocks.note: [PD] Traditional Compline opening versicle 'The Lord Almighty grant us a quiet night and a perfect end' (medieval origin; PD in English use). ELW's contemporary 'quiet night and peace at the last' wording is [LIC], resolved via entitlement.

[PD] the 1662 BCP General Confession, bundled as the PUBLIC-DOMAIN SUBSTITUTE for ELW's Compline confession (doc 10 flag #3: a working substitute, NOT verbatim ELW). ELW's own confession wording is [LIC], resolved via entitlement.

blocks.title: Psalm
blocks.note: [VAR] The traditional Compline psalms are Psalm 91 and/or Psalm 134; the day's appointment may vary. Text/number from the day's appointment (S&S / lectionary).

blocks.title: *OFFICE HYMN
blocks.note: [VAR] Optional office hymn ('Song'), sung between the Psalmody and the Reading per the S&S ordo. (S&S also allows an optional 'Night Hymn' before the Confession; not modeled here.) render mode is per-document renderer-side.

blocks.title: READING
blocks.note: [VAR] A short appointed lesson. The response is a renderer-side inline literal.

blocks.title: RESPONSORY
blocks.note: [PD] the traditional Compline responsory 'Into thy hands, O Lord, I commend my spirit' (Ps 31:5 / Luke 23:46), public domain.
[PD] Nunc Dimittis, Luke 2:29-32, King James Version (public domain). The bundled traditional-language default; ELW's ELLC contemporary canticle is [LIC], resolved via entitlement.

blocks.title: INTERCESSIONS
blocks.note: [VAR/LIC] The Night Prayer intercessions (the opening verse 'Hear my prayer, O Lord' + the appointed prayer per the S&S ordo), prayed before the Lord's Prayer. ELW's specific wording is copyrighted and resolves via the church's Augsburg/S&S license; no ELW text is bundled. Replaces the spurious threefold Kyrie the draft previously carried over from the Communion rite (no Kyrie in the S&S Night Prayer ordo).

blocks.title: *LORD'S PRAYER
blocks.note: [PD] Reuses elw.lords_prayer, whose constant is the traditional 'Our Father, who art in heaven' wording (public domain).

blocks.title: COLLECT
blocks.note: [VAR/LIC] The appointed Compline collect(s) (S&S / ELW propers); resolves via entitlement. No PD fallback bundled.

blocks.title: *BLESSING
blocks.note: [PD] Concluding blessing; falls back to the traditional Compline versicle 'The Lord Almighty grant us a quiet night and a perfect end' (pd.compline_open). ELW's own blessing wording is [LIC] and, when entitled, resolves over this PD default.
